macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US Inflation Eases in June, Dampening Fed Rate Hike Bets and Boosting Global Stocks
items.summary: US consumer prices dropped in June due to falling energy costs, leading Asian and US stocks to climb as investors scaled back expectations for aggressive Federal Reserve rate hikes; the dollar weakened following the softer data.

items.title: China's Q2 Economic Growth Slows to 4.3%, Missing Expectations Amid Weak Demand
items.summary: China's annual economic growth fell sharply to 4.3% in the second quarter of 2026, missing analyst forecasts as weak domestic demand and oil shocks from the Iran war outweighed strong exports and production.

items.title: Oil Hits One-Month High Near $85 as US-Iran Tensions Cloud Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: Brent crude rose to nearly $85 a barrel amid renewed hostilities between the US and Iran, though prices took a breather after the US scrapped a plan to levy shipping fees through the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: NVIDIA Surges 4%, IBM Plunges 25% in Major 24-Hour Stock Movers
items.summary: NVIDIA Corporation gained 4.06% while IBM dropped 25.21% in the past 24 hours; the S&P 500 rose 0.38%, and JPMorgan Chase climbed 2.50% following strong market sentiment.

items.title: ASML Raises AI Chip Guidance Again After Blowout Quarter
items.summary: ASML Holding raised its guidance for the second time this year after delivering a blowout quarter, driven by customers ramping up production capacity for AI chips.

items.title: Morgan Stanley Posts Record Quarterly Revenue as Equities Trading Surges 69%
items.summary: Morgan Stanley reported record quarterly revenue and profit, fueled by a 69% surge in equities trading volume as market volatility and investor activity increased.

items.title: India-Britain Trade Pact Takes Effect, Cutting Tariffs on Thousands of Goods
items.summary: A comprehensive trade pact between India and Britain officially took effect on Wednesday, reducing tariffs and widening access for services firms and professionals in both markets.

items.title: South Korea's KOSPI Enters Bear Market After 25% Drop from June Highs
items.summary: South Korea's KOSPI index has entered a bear market after falling approximately 25% from its record high reached in late June, marking a sharp reversal of one of the world's strongest equity rallies.

items.title: Bank of Canada Expected to Hold Rates Steady Amid Contained Inflation Concerns
items.summary: The Bank of Canada is widely expected to keep its benchmark rate unchanged on Wednesday as concerns over a recent inflation spike remain contained and economic growth stays subdued.

items.title: Brazil Braces for New 25% US Tariff on Imports After Unproductive Negotiations
items.summary: Brazil is preparing for the US to impose a new 25% tariff on thousands of its imports following months of largely unproductive negotiations, a move expected to trigger broader US tariffs on multiple countries.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Surges Above $64,000 as Soft Inflation Data Dims Rate Hike Odds
items.summary: Bitcoin gained approximately 3.5% over the past 24 hours, hitting $64,752 and briefly touching $65,000, while Ethereum rose 4.72% to $1,876 following cooler-than-expected U.S. inflation numbers that reduced Federal Reserve rate hike expectations[2][12].

items.title: XRP and Dogecoin Join Market Rally Amid Geopolitical Tensions
items.summary: XRP jumped 3.52% to $1.10 and Dogecoin rose 2.93% to $77.59 as leading cryptocurrencies rallied alongside stocks, despite escalating U.S.-Iran military tensions that boosted oil prices but weighed on haven assets[2][6].

items.title: Over $350 Million Liquidated in Crypto Market as Bearish Shorts Suffer
items.summary: The cryptocurrency market saw over $350 million in liquidations within 24 hours, with bearish short traders bearing the brunt of losses, while Bitcoin's open interest rose 2.09%[2].

items.title: New York Proposes Stablecoin Regulations to Align with GENIUS Act
items.summary: The New York State Department of Financial Services proposed new regulations to update and strengthen its stablecoin rules, aiming to align with the federal GENIUS Act framework[1].

items.title: U.S. and UK Strengthen Digital Asset Partnership with Tokenized Finance Roadmap
items.summary: The United States and United Kingdom introduced a new roadmap focused on regulated stablecoins and tokenized finance, strengthening their partnership on digital assets[3][5].

items.title: Tether Freezes $131 Million USDT Linked to Iranian Addresses
items.summary: Tether froze $131 million in USDT on addresses associated with Iran, continuing its compliance efforts amid escalating U.S.-Iran tensions[5].

items.title: NovaBox Loses Nearly 99.86% of ETH Reward Pool in Flash Loan Exploit
items.summary: A flash loan attack drained nearly all funds from NovaBox's reward pool on Ethereum, resulting in losses of approximately 56.73 ETH[1].

items.title: THORChain Advances Recovery with v3.19.0 Security Upgrade
items.summary: THORChain is entering a critical recovery phase following its May security incident, with validators reviewing and implementing the v3.19.0 security upgrade[1].

items.title: Ripple Achieves Full MiCA Compliance for EU-Wide Crypto Payments
items.summary: Ripple gained full compliance with the EU's Markets in Crypto-Assets (MiCA) regulation, paving the way for expanded crypto payment services across Europe[7].

items.title: SBI Shinsei Offers Crypto Vouchers for Bitcoin, Ethereum, and XRP
items.summary: Japanese bank SBI Shinsei launched a program offering depositors vouchers exchangeable for Bitcoin, Ethereum, or Ripple within a specified period at prevailing market rates[1].

items.title: U.S. Government Transfers $288 Million Bitcoin and Ethereum to Coinbase Prime
items.summary: The U.S. government transferred approximately $288 million worth of Bitcoin and Ethereum to Coinbase Prime, marking a significant movement of seized or held digital assets[8].

items.title: Tether Invests $20 Million in Mercado Bitcoin for Latin American Innovation
items.summary: Tether announced a $20 million investment in Mercado Bitcoin to accelerate blockchain innovation and adoption in Latin America[7].

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: OpenAI Releases GPT-5.6 Series to General Availability with Three Configurations
items.summary: OpenAI has moved its new GPT-5.6 series to general availability, featuring three configurations: the flagship Sol, balanced Terra, and low-cost Luna; the Sol Ultra variant recently solved the 50-year-old Cycle Double Cover math conjecture in under an hour.

items.title: SpaceXAI (xAI) Launches Grok 4.5 with 1.5 Trillion Parameters
items.summary: SpaceXAI (formerly xAI) released Grok 4.5 to the public, boasting a 1.5 trillion parameter count and pricing at $2 per million input tokens and $6 per million output tokens.

items.title: Meta Introduces Muse Spark 1.1 Multimodal Reasoning Model from Superintelligence Labs
items.summary: Meta launched Muse Spark 1.1, a multimodal reasoning model from Superintelligence Labs that highlights significant improvements in agent behavior and coding capabilities.

items.title: SambaNova Systems Raises $1B in Series F, Reaching $11B Valuation
items.summary: AI semiconductor company SambaNova Systems secured $1 billion in the first close of its Series F, achieving a post-money valuation of $11 billion amid rising demand for AI hardware.

items.title: Meta Plans 5-Gigawatt 'Hyperion' Data Center in Louisiana with $50B Investment
items.summary: Meta is expanding its 'Hyperion' data center in Richland Parish, Louisiana, to 5 gigawatts of computing power with an investment exceeding $50 billion to support its AI infrastructure.

items.title: Anthropic Lifts US Restrictions on Latest Claude Versions and Launches 'Claude for Teachers'
items.summary: The Trump administration lifted restrictions on Anthropic's latest Claude chatbot versions after a cybersecurity review, while Anthropic simultaneously launched 'Claude for Teachers' for verified US K-12 educators.

items.title: Apple Intelligence Registered for Use in China Following Regulatory Approval
items.summary: China's cyberspace regulator registered Apple's on-device generative AI service 'Apple Intelligence' for use on iPhones in the country, marking a key step in its global rollout.

items.title: New York Blocks New Large AI Data Centers for Up to One Year
items.summary: New York announced a one-year block on new large data centers fueling artificial intelligence to protect the environment and energy grid from strain.

items.title: Alibaba Releases Real-Time Voice Interaction Model Qwen-Audio-3.0-Realtime
items.summary: Alibaba launched Qwen-Audio-3.0-Realtime, a real-time voice interaction model that upgrades capabilities in intelligence, agent tool calling, empathetic conversation, and duplex interaction fluency.

items.title: Fujitsu Announces 'Kozuchi Multi AI Agent Framework' for Self-Evolving Agents
items.summary: Fujitsu announced the 'Kozuchi Multi AI Agent Framework,' a platform for developing and operating self-evolving multi-AI agents that autonomously optimize business processes.

items.title: PixVerse Secures $439M Funding, Valuing Video-Generation Startup at Over $2B
items.summary: Video-generation startup PixVerse secured $439 million in funding, raising its valuation to over $2 billion as demand for AI video tools surges.

items.title: Google Unveils Expanded Gemini Enterprise Portfolio at Google Cloud Next '26
items.summary: At Google Cloud Next '26, Google unveiled an expanded Gemini Enterprise portfolio, a unified platform for building, orchestrating, and governing AI agents across organizations.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Palworld Surges Past 850,000 Players on Steam After Version 1.0 Release
items.summary: Palworld experienced a massive spike in concurrent players, exceeding 850,000 on Steam following the launch of its Version 1.0 update, marking a significant milestone for the survival game.

items.title: GTA VI Price Debate Intensifies as Analyst Predicts $200 Premium Edition
items.summary: An analyst's claim that GTA VI could be sold for up to $200 has reignited industry controversy over game pricing, premium editions, and monetization strategies ahead of the game's release.

items.title: Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced DLCs Generate $1 Million Despite $85 Cost
items.summary: The controversial launch DLCs for Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced, which cost 85 euros collectively, have already generated $1 million in revenue on Steam, surpassing the base game's price.

items.title: Ubisoft Barcelona Staff Strike Over Layoffs Following Black Flag Resynced Launch
items.summary: Workers at Ubisoft Barcelona have gone on strike to protest the layoff of 51 staff members, which occurred shortly after the release of Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced.

items.title: Sony indefinitely Delays FlexStrike Arcade Controller for PS5 and PC
items.summary: Sony has postponed the launch of its FlexStrike wireless arcade controller indefinitely due to unexpected production issues, following a previous delay from July to August.

items.title: Dying Light: The Beast PS4 and Xbox One Versions Cancelled
items.summary: Techland has cancelled the PS4 and Xbox One versions of Dying Light: The Beast, citing technical limitations that would require excessive cuts to the game experience.

items.title: Epic Games Gives Away Horror Game 'Together After Dark' for Free
items.summary: The Epic Games Store has launched a free giveaway for the horror game Together After Dark, available to claim until July 27, 2026.

items.title: Forza Horizon 6 Leaderboards Wiped to Patch Exploits
items.summary: Playground Games has wiped all Forza Horizon 6 leaderboards after patching out exploits, stating the action is necessary to ensure everyone starts with a 'clean sheet'.

items.title: Ex-Xbox Studio Compulsion Games Goes Independent and Seeks Partners
items.summary: Former Xbox studio Compulsion Games has invited partners to collaborate after officially going independent following its closure by Xbox.

items.title: Castlevania: Belmont's Curse Confirmed for Switch Release in October
items.summary: Castlevania: Belmont's Curse will launch on Nintendo Switch on October 15, 2026, alongside PS5, Xbox Series, and PC versions.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Apple Sues OpenAI for Trade Secret Theft Over Unveiled AI Gadgets
items.summary: Apple filed a bombshell lawsuit against OpenAI and two former employees, alleging misappropriation of trade secrets to develop its own hardware for ChatGPT, sending shock waves through Silicon Valley.

items.title: ASML Raises 2026 Sales Forecast and Plans Capacity Expansion
items.summary: ASML, the world's largest chip equipment supplier, increased its 2026 financial forecast for the second time and announced capacity expansion following better-than-expected Q2 earnings driven by global chip demand.

items.title: Drone Startup Neros Secures Potential $500 Million U.S. Army Deal
items.summary: Drone startup Neros reportedly secured a deal with the U.S. Army that could be worth up to $500 million, marking a significant milestone for the company.

items.title: Amazon Invests Billions in Warehouse Robotics
items.summary: Amazon is investing billions of dollars into warehouse robotics, a move with significant implications for the robotics sector and competitors like Symbotic.

items.title: Google Challenges Nvidia's Chip Dominance by Pitching TPUs to Cloud Partners
items.summary: Google is actively pitching its Tensor Processing Units (TPUs) to neoclouds and cloud startups to directly challenge Nvidia's stronghold in the computing market.

items.title: Canva Code 2.0 with Vibe Coding Support Now Available to All
items.summary: Canva has released Code 2.0, featuring Vibe Coding support for website building, now available to all users for creating websites directly within the platform.

items.title: Boston Dynamics' 'Spot' Robot Enters Talks with Major Logistics Companies
items.summary: Boston Dynamics is in talks with major logistics companies to deploy its four-legged 'Spot' robot to assist human delivery workers in logistics operations.

items.title: Telegram's t.me Domain Suspended Amid US Treasury Crackdown
items.summary: Telegram's t.me domain was suspended by a top-level domain provider to comply with a US Treasury crackdown on a VPN service used by ransomware groups.

items.title: IBM Sparks Market Rout with Revenue Forecast Below Estimates
items.summary: IBM triggered a market rout after forecasting second-quarter revenue below estimates, signaling that businesses are prioritizing data-center infrastructure spending over software.

items.title: New York Blocks New Large Data Centers for Up to One Year
items.summary: New York will block the construction of new large data centers for up to a year to protect the environment and energy grid, though this move is linked to AI infrastructure concerns.
